description: Pho bo is a Vietnamese beef noodle soup built on a long-simmered broth with rice noodles, sliced beef, herbs, and lime.
steps: Simmer beef bones with charred onion, ginger, and spices until the broth is rich and clear.
Season the broth with fish sauce and a little sugar to balance the flavor.
Cook the rice noodles separately and place them in serving bowls.
Top with thin sliced beef, ladle over boiling broth, and finish with herbs, bean sprouts, and lime.

description: Pho ga is a lighter Vietnamese noodle soup made with chicken broth, rice noodles, shredded chicken, and fresh herbs.
steps: Poach the chicken with onion and ginger, then remove the meat and strain the broth.
Season the broth with fish sauce and keep it hot.
Cook the rice noodles and divide them into bowls with shredded chicken.
Pour over the broth and serve with scallions, cilantro, and lime.

description: Bun cha combines grilled pork patties and pork belly with rice noodles, herbs, and a light sweet-savory dipping broth.
steps: Marinate the pork belly and ground pork with fish sauce, sugar, garlic, and shallot.
Grill the pork until caramelized and lightly charred.
Mix a dipping broth with fish sauce, vinegar, sugar, water, and pickled carrot.
Serve the grilled pork with rice vermicelli, herbs, and the dipping broth.

description: Banh mi thit is a Vietnamese baguette sandwich layered with savory meat, pickles, herbs, and creamy condiments.
steps: Split and lightly toast the baguette.
Spread the inside with mayonnaise and pate.
Layer in the meat, pickles, cucumber, cilantro, and chili if desired.
Close the sandwich and serve while the bread is still crisp.

description: Goi cuon are fresh Vietnamese spring rolls filled with shrimp, pork, herbs, and rice noodles, usually served with peanut sauce.
steps: Soften a sheet of rice paper in warm water until flexible.
Layer lettuce, herbs, noodles, pork, and shrimp in the center.
Roll tightly, folding in the sides to form a neat fresh roll.
Serve the rolls whole or halved with peanut dipping sauce.

description: Bun bo Hue is a spicy central Vietnamese noodle soup with beef, pork, lemongrass, and a deep chili-laced broth.
steps: Simmer beef and pork with lemongrass until the broth is full-bodied.
Season with shrimp paste, fish sauce, and chili oil for the signature flavor.
Cook the noodles and arrange them in bowls with sliced meat.
Ladle over the hot broth and serve with herbs, lime, and shredded banana blossom if available.

description: Com tam is broken rice served with grilled pork, pickles, and a fish sauce dressing, often with egg or pork skin.
steps: Marinate the pork chop with fish sauce, garlic, sugar, and pepper.
Grill or pan-sear the pork until cooked through and caramelized.
Cook the broken rice and prepare pickles and scallion oil.
Plate the rice with pork, pickles, egg, and a spoon of fish sauce dressing.

description: Banh xeo is a crisp turmeric rice crepe folded over pork, shrimp, and bean sprouts and eaten with herbs and lettuce.
steps: Mix the batter and rest it briefly so it pours evenly.
Cook shrimp and pork in a hot skillet, then add a thin layer of batter.
Scatter in bean sprouts and cook until the crepe is crisp.
Fold the crepe and serve with lettuce, herbs, and dipping sauce.

description: Mi Quang is a central Vietnamese noodle dish with turmeric noodles, a small amount of rich broth, and toppings like shrimp or chicken.
steps: Cook the protein with shallot, turmeric, and seasonings.
Add a small amount of stock so the sauce-broth stays concentrated.
Warm the noodles and place them in bowls.
Top with the protein, broth, herbs, peanuts, and pieces of rice cracker.

description: Hu tieu Nam Vang is a southern noodle soup with a clear pork-based broth and toppings such as shrimp, pork, and quail eggs.
steps: Simmer pork bones to make a light but savory broth.
Cook shrimp, pork, and quail eggs for the toppings.
Blanch the noodles and place them in serving bowls.
Top with the cooked ingredients, pour over broth, and finish with garlic oil and herbs.

description: Bun rieu is a Vietnamese crab and tomato noodle soup known for its tangy broth and soft crab-pork patties.
steps: Cook tomatoes until soft to form the base of the broth.
Add stock and season with fish sauce and a little shrimp paste if using.
Drop in the crab and pork mixture so it cooks into tender clusters.
Serve over noodles with tofu, herbs, and chili paste.

description: Ca kho to is caramelized braised fish cooked in a clay pot with fish sauce, sugar, and black pepper.
steps: Make a light caramel in the pot, then add garlic and shallot.
Add the fish with fish sauce and coconut water.
Simmer gently until the sauce reduces and coats the fish.
Finish with black pepper and serve with steamed rice.

description: Canh chua ca is a sweet-sour fish soup flavored with tamarind, pineapple, tomatoes, and herbs.
steps: Bring stock to a simmer and dissolve tamarind into the broth.
Add pineapple and tomatoes to build the sweet-sour base.
Poach the fish until just cooked through.
Add vegetables and herbs at the end and serve hot with rice.

description: Bo luc lac is a quick stir-fry of seared beef cubes with onion, pepper, and a savory soy-fish sauce glaze.
steps: Marinate the beef cubes briefly with soy sauce, fish sauce, garlic, and sugar.
Sear the beef over high heat so the outside browns but the center stays tender.
Add onion and toss until just softened.
Serve with watercress or rice, and a lime-pepper dipping sauce if desired.

description: Cha ca La Vong is Hanoi-style turmeric fish served with dill, scallions, peanuts, and rice noodles.
steps: Marinate the fish with turmeric, aromatics, and seasonings.
Pan-fry the fish until lightly charred and cooked through.
Add dill and scallions at the end so they wilt in the hot pan.
Serve with noodles, peanuts, herbs, and dipping sauce.

description: Nem ran are crisp Vietnamese fried spring rolls filled with pork, mushrooms, and vegetables.
steps: Mix the pork with soaked mushrooms, glass noodles, carrot, and seasoning.
Wrap the filling tightly in spring roll wrappers.
Fry the rolls until golden and crisp.
Serve with herbs, lettuce, and nuoc cham.

description: Bun thit nuong is a southern Vietnamese noodle bowl with grilled pork, rice vermicelli, herbs, and fish sauce dressing.
steps: Marinate sliced pork with lemongrass, fish sauce, sugar, and garlic.
Grill or pan-sear the pork until browned and aromatic.
Cook the noodles and prepare herbs and pickled vegetables.
Assemble the bowl and spoon over nuoc cham before serving.

description: Xoi ga is sticky rice topped with seasoned chicken and often finished with fried shallots and scallion oil.
steps: Soak and steam the sticky rice until tender.
Cook the chicken with savory seasoning until done, then shred or slice it.
Prepare scallion oil and fried shallots for topping.
Serve the sticky rice with chicken and garnish generously.

description: Chao ga is a comforting Vietnamese chicken rice porridge made with gently cooked rice and shredded chicken.
steps: Simmer chicken in water or stock, then remove the meat and shred it.
Cook the rice in the broth until it breaks down into porridge.
Season lightly with fish sauce and ginger.
Return the chicken to the bowl and top with scallions and fried shallots.

description: Sup cua is a silky Vietnamese crab soup often thickened with egg whites and served as a light starter.
steps: Bring the stock to a gentle simmer and season it lightly.
Thicken the soup with a small cornstarch slurry.
Add crab meat and stream in egg whites so they form wisps.
Finish with quail eggs, cilantro, and white pepper.

description: Kung Pao chicken is a Sichuan stir-fry of marinated chicken, dried chilies, peanuts, and a balanced sweet-savory sauce.
steps: Marinate the chicken with soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and starch.
Toast the chilies and Sichuan peppercorns briefly in hot oil with aromatics.
Stir-fry the chicken until nearly cooked, then add the sauce.
Finish with peanuts and toss until glossy.

description: Mapo tofu is a spicy, numbing Sichuan dish of soft tofu and minced meat in a chili bean sauce.
steps: Brown the pork and fry the doubanjiang with garlic and ginger.
Add stock and season the sauce.
Slide in the tofu cubes and simmer gently so they stay intact.
Thicken lightly and finish with ground Sichuan pepper and scallions.

description: Sweet and sour pork combines crisp fried pork with a glossy sauce made from vinegar, sugar, and ketchup or tomato.
steps: Coat and fry the pork pieces until crisp.
Stir-fry the peppers and pineapple briefly.
Simmer the sweet and sour sauce until slightly thickened.
Add the pork back at the end so it stays crisp in the sauce.

description: Fried rice is a quick wok dish built from cold cooked rice, egg, aromatics, and small pieces of meat or vegetables.
steps: Break up the cold rice so the grains separate easily.
Scramble the egg and set it aside.
Stir-fry aromatics and vegetables, then add the rice over high heat.
Season, return the egg, and toss until hot and dry.

description: Chow mein is a stir-fried noodle dish with vegetables and protein tossed in a savory soy-based sauce.
steps: Par-cook the noodles and drain them well.
Stir-fry the protein and vegetables in a hot wok.
Add the noodles and sauce and toss until evenly coated.
Cook briefly until the noodles pick up a little char.

description: Hot and sour soup is a Chinese soup flavored with vinegar, white pepper, mushrooms, tofu, and silky egg ribbons.
steps: Simmer the stock with mushrooms and tofu.
Season with soy sauce, vinegar, and white pepper for the hot-sour balance.
Thicken the soup lightly with starch.
Drizzle in beaten egg and finish with sesame oil and scallions.

description: Peking duck is roast duck with lacquered skin traditionally served with pancakes, scallions, and sweet bean sauce.
steps: Air-dry the duck after glazing the skin so it roasts crisp.
Roast until the skin is deep golden and the meat is tender.
Rest the duck and carve the skin and meat into slices.
Serve with pancakes, scallions, cucumber, and sauce.

description: Char siu is Cantonese barbecued pork known for its sweet-savory glaze and red-burnished exterior.
steps: Marinate the pork well so the flavor penetrates the meat.
Roast or grill until the pork is cooked and caramelized at the edges.
Baste with glaze during the final stage for shine.
Rest briefly, then slice thinly.

description: Chinese dumplings are filled dough parcels usually stuffed with pork, chive, or cabbage and then boiled, steamed, or pan-fried.
steps: Mix the filling until sticky and well seasoned.
Place a small spoonful in each wrapper and seal the dumplings tightly.
Cook by boiling, steaming, or pan-frying until done.
Serve with black vinegar and soy dipping sauce.

description: Wonton soup pairs delicate meat-filled wontons with a clear broth and green vegetables.
steps: Mix the wonton filling and wrap the wontons carefully.
Bring the broth to a simmer with ginger and seasoning.
Cook the wontons until they float and the filling is cooked.
Serve in hot broth with greens and a few drops of sesame oil.

description: Dan dan noodles are Sichuan noodles tossed with a spicy sesame-chili sauce and topped with seasoned minced pork.
steps: Cook the minced pork with soy sauce and preserved vegetables.
Whisk together sesame paste, chili oil, soy sauce, and stock for the sauce.
Boil the noodles and drain them well.
Combine noodles with sauce and top with pork and scallions.

description: Egg drop soup is a simple Chinese soup with seasoned broth and silky strands of beaten egg.
steps: Heat the stock and season with salt, white pepper, and sesame oil.
Thicken very lightly with starch if desired.
Slowly drizzle in beaten eggs while stirring the broth.
Finish with scallions and serve immediately.

description: Scallion pancakes are layered Chinese flatbreads crisped in a skillet with fragrant green onion between the folds.
steps: Make a soft dough and rest it so it rolls easily.
Roll out the dough, brush with oil, and scatter on scallions and salt.
Coil and reroll the dough to create flaky layers.
Pan-fry until both sides are golden and crisp.

description: Salt and pepper shrimp is a quick stir-fry or fry of shell-on shrimp seasoned with garlic, chili, and aromatic pepper salt.
steps: Coat the shrimp lightly and fry or sear until nearly cooked.
Cook garlic and chili until fragrant.
Toss the shrimp with the aromatics and pepper-salt mixture.
Serve hot while the shells are still crisp.

description: Braised eggplant in the Chinese style becomes silky and savory with garlic, soy sauce, and a little sugar.
steps: Cook the eggplant until soft and lightly browned.
Stir-fry garlic briefly and add the sauce ingredients.
Return the eggplant to the pan and braise until glazed.
Finish with scallions and serve with rice.

description: Steamed fish with ginger is a Cantonese dish that highlights delicate fish with soy, ginger, and hot oil over scallions.
steps: Steam the fish until just cooked through.
Scatter ginger and scallions over the fish.
Pour over soy sauce and a splash of hot oil to release the aroma.
Serve immediately with steamed rice.

description: General Tso chicken is a Chinese American favorite of crisp fried chicken in a sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy glaze.
steps: Coat and fry the chicken until crisp.
Simmer the sauce ingredients until glossy and lightly thickened.
Toss the fried chicken in the sauce just before serving.
Serve with rice so the coating stays as crisp as possible.

description: Beef and broccoli is a Cantonese-inspired stir-fry of tender beef slices, broccoli, and a savory brown sauce.
steps: Slice and marinate the beef with soy sauce and starch.
Blanch or steam the broccoli until bright green.
Stir-fry the beef quickly over high heat with garlic.
Add the broccoli and sauce and toss until the beef is just cooked.

description: Ma la xiang guo is a dry pot stir-fry built from mixed vegetables, meats, and tofu coated in a spicy mala seasoning base.
steps: Prepare the ingredients in bite-size pieces so they cook evenly.
Cook the proteins and firmer vegetables first.
Fry the mala base with aromatics to bloom the spices.
Toss everything together until coated and fragrant.

description: Lo mein is a saucy noodle stir-fry where cooked noodles are tossed with vegetables and protein in a savory sauce.
steps: Cook the noodles until just tender and drain well.
Stir-fry the protein and vegetables in a hot wok.
Add the noodles and sauce and toss to coat evenly.
Finish with sesame oil and serve immediately.

description: Sushi rolls are seasoned rice and fillings wrapped in nori, then sliced into bite-size pieces.
steps: Cook and season the sushi rice, then cool it to room temperature.
Spread rice over nori and arrange the fillings in a line.
Roll tightly using a bamboo mat.
Slice with a damp knife and serve with soy sauce, pickled ginger, and wasabi.

description: Salmon nigiri is hand-formed sushi rice topped with slices of salmon and served in small individual pieces.
steps: Cook and season the sushi rice, then cool it slightly.
Slice the salmon into even sashimi-style pieces.
Shape small ovals of rice by hand.
Place salmon on top, optionally dab with wasabi, and serve.

description: Chicken katsu is a crisp panko-breaded chicken cutlet usually served with shredded cabbage and tonkatsu sauce.
steps: Flatten and season the chicken evenly.
Coat it in flour, egg, and panko.
Fry until golden brown and cooked through.
Slice and serve with cabbage and sauce.

description: Tonkotsu ramen is a rich noodle soup built on a creamy pork bone broth with noodles, chashu, and toppings.
steps: Boil and clean the bones, then simmer them for hours until the broth turns milky.
Prepare the tare and toppings separately.
Cook the ramen noodles just before serving.
Combine tare, broth, noodles, and toppings in each bowl.

description: Shoyu ramen is a soy-based ramen with a clear savory broth, springy noodles, and classic toppings.
steps: Prepare a clear stock and a soy-based tare.
Warm the toppings so everything is ready to assemble.
Cook the ramen noodles until springy.
Add tare and broth to the bowl, then the noodles and toppings.

description: Udon soup is a Japanese noodle soup with thick wheat noodles in a light dashi-based broth.
steps: Bring dashi, soy sauce, and mirin to a simmer.
Cook the udon noodles until tender.
Place the noodles in bowls and pour over the broth.
Top with scallions and any chosen garnishes.

description: Soba noodles are often served simply with a dipping sauce or light broth to highlight their earthy buckwheat flavor.
steps: Cook the soba noodles and rinse them well to remove excess starch.
Prepare the dipping sauce or broth with dashi, soy sauce, and mirin.
Arrange the noodles neatly for serving.
Serve with scallions, nori, and dipping sauce or broth.

description: Tempura is seafood or vegetables coated in a light batter and fried until delicate and crisp.
steps: Prepare the seafood and vegetables so they are dry and ready to fry.
Mix a very cold tempura batter without overworking it.
Fry the ingredients in batches until pale golden and crisp.
Drain briefly and serve at once with dipping sauce.

description: Okonomiyaki is a savory Japanese pancake made with cabbage, batter, and toppings like pork or seafood.
steps: Mix the batter with shredded cabbage and other fillings.
Pan-cook the pancake until both sides are deeply golden.
Steam briefly if needed so the center cooks through.
Finish with sauce, mayonnaise, seaweed flakes, and bonito flakes.

description: Takoyaki are round batter balls filled with octopus, cooked in a special pan and topped with sauce and bonito flakes.
steps: Heat the takoyaki pan well and oil the molds generously.
Pour in the batter and add octopus and toppings.
Turn the balls repeatedly as they set and brown.
Top with sauce, mayonnaise, bonito flakes, and aonori.

description: Yakitori is Japanese grilled chicken skewers seasoned with either salt or a tare glaze.
steps: Thread chicken and scallions onto skewers.
Make a tare glaze with soy sauce, mirin, and sugar.
Grill the skewers while brushing with tare as they cook.
Serve hot straight from the grill.

description: Gyudon is a Japanese beef rice bowl with thin beef and onions simmered in a sweet-savory sauce.
steps: Simmer onion in dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar.
Add the beef and cook briefly until tender.
Spoon hot rice into bowls.
Top the rice with the beef and onion mixture and any preferred garnish.

description: Oyakodon is a comforting rice bowl of chicken and onion bound with softly set egg.
steps: Simmer chicken and onion in a lightly sweetened dashi broth.
Pour in beaten eggs and cook until softly set.
Place hot rice in a bowl.
Slide the chicken and egg mixture over the rice and serve immediately.

description: Karaage is Japanese fried chicken marinated with soy, ginger, and garlic, then fried until juicy and crisp.
steps: Marinate the chicken with soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and sake if available.
Coat the chicken in potato starch.
Fry until crisp and cooked through, often in two stages for extra crunch.
Drain well and serve with lemon.

description: Miso soup is a simple Japanese soup of dashi and dissolved miso, often with tofu and wakame.
steps: Heat the dashi gently without boiling hard.
Add tofu and wakame and warm through.
Dissolve the miso in a little broth and stir it back into the pot.
Finish with scallions and serve immediately.

description: Onigiri are Japanese rice balls shaped by hand and often filled with salmon, tuna, or pickled plum.
steps: Cook the rice and let it cool enough to handle.
Wet and salt your hands, then shape rice around the filling.
Form into triangles or rounds.
Wrap with nori just before serving.

description: Teriyaki salmon is pan-seared or broiled salmon glazed with a sweet soy-mirin sauce.
steps: Sear or broil the salmon until nearly cooked.
Simmer the teriyaki sauce until slightly syrupy.
Brush or spoon the sauce over the fish and glaze briefly.
Serve with rice and vegetables.

description: Unagi don is a rice bowl topped with grilled eel lacquered in a sweet soy-based tare.
steps: Warm the eel and brush with tare as it heats.
Cook or reheat the rice for serving.
Lay the eel over the rice and spoon on extra sauce.
Finish with sansho pepper if desired.

description: Chawanmushi is a savory steamed egg custard flavored with dashi and often filled with shrimp, chicken, or mushrooms.
steps: Whisk eggs gently with seasoned dashi.
Place the fillings in small cups.
Pour in the egg mixture and strain if needed for a smooth texture.
Steam gently until the custard is just set.

description: Nikujaga is a home-style Japanese stew of beef, potatoes, and onions simmered in a sweet-savory broth.
steps: Cook the beef and onion briefly in a little oil.
Add potatoes and the seasoned dashi.
Simmer until the potatoes are tender and the broth is reduced.
Serve warm as a comforting side or main dish.

description: Margherita pizza is a classic Neapolitan-style pizza topped simply with tomato, mozzarella, basil, and olive oil.
steps: Stretch the dough into a round on a floured surface.
Spread on tomato sauce and add torn mozzarella.
Bake in a very hot oven until the crust blisters.
Finish with basil and olive oil before serving.

description: Spaghetti carbonara is a Roman pasta dish made with eggs, cheese, cured pork, and black pepper, without cream.
steps: Cook the pasta and reserve starchy pasta water.
Render the guanciale until crisp and the fat is flavorful.
Mix egg yolks, cheese, and pepper in a bowl.
Toss the hot pasta with the pork and egg mixture off the heat, loosening with pasta water.

description: Lasagna is layered pasta baked with meat sauce, bechamel or cheese, and a browned top.
steps: Cook the ragu until thick and flavorful.
Prepare the white sauce or cheese layer.
Layer pasta, sauce, and cheese in a baking dish.
Bake until bubbling and browned, then rest before slicing.

description: Risotto ai funghi is a creamy Italian rice dish slowly cooked with stock and mushrooms.
steps: Saute the mushrooms and set aside part for topping if desired.
Cook shallot and toast the rice in butter or oil.
Add wine, then ladle in hot stock gradually while stirring.
Finish with mushrooms, butter, and parmesan for a creamy texture.

description: Penne arrabbiata is pasta in a spicy tomato sauce flavored with garlic, chili, and olive oil.
steps: Cook the penne until al dente.
Gently saute garlic and chili in olive oil.
Add tomatoes and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Toss with the pasta and serve hot.

description: Fettuccine Alfredo is pasta coated in a rich sauce made from butter, cheese, and pasta water, often with cream in modern versions.
steps: Cook the pasta and reserve some cooking water.
Warm the butter and, if using, cream gently.
Add the pasta and toss with parmesan until a sauce forms.
Adjust with pasta water and pepper before serving.

description: Gnocchi al pesto pairs soft potato dumplings with basil pesto and a little pasta water or butter.
steps: Boil the gnocchi until they float.
Loosen the pesto with a little cooking water.
Toss the gnocchi gently in the sauce.
Serve with parmesan and extra basil.

description: Minestrone is an Italian vegetable soup that often includes beans, pasta, and seasonal vegetables.
steps: Cook the soffritto until softened and aromatic.
Add vegetables, beans, tomatoes, and stock and simmer.
Add pasta toward the end so it stays tender but not overcooked.
Serve with olive oil and parmesan if desired.

description: Osso buco is a Milanese-style braise of veal shanks cooked until tender in wine, stock, and aromatics.
steps: Brown the veal shanks well on both sides.
Cook the soffritto and deglaze with white wine.
Braise the shanks gently in stock until fork tender.
Finish with gremolata before serving.

description: Chicken cacciatore is a rustic Italian braise of chicken with tomatoes, wine, herbs, and vegetables.
steps: Brown the chicken pieces and set them aside.
Cook the vegetables until softened.
Add wine and tomatoes, then return the chicken to simmer.
Cook until the sauce is rich and the chicken is tender.

description: Bruschetta is grilled bread rubbed with garlic and topped with chopped tomato, basil, and olive oil.
steps: Toast or grill slices of bread until crisp.
Rub the warm bread lightly with garlic.
Mix tomatoes with basil, olive oil, and seasoning.
Spoon the tomato mixture over the bread just before serving.

description: Caprese salad is a simple Italian salad of ripe tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, basil, and olive oil.
steps: Slice the tomatoes and mozzarella evenly.
Arrange them in overlapping layers on a plate.
Tuck in basil leaves between the slices.
Season with salt and drizzle with olive oil.

description: Eggplant Parmigiana is layered fried or baked eggplant with tomato sauce, mozzarella, parmesan, and basil.
steps: Salt and cook the eggplant slices until tender.
Prepare or warm the tomato sauce.
Layer eggplant, sauce, and cheeses in a baking dish.
Bake until bubbling and golden on top.

description: Seafood linguine is pasta tossed with shellfish or mixed seafood in a tomato or white wine sauce.
steps: Cook the pasta until nearly done.
Saute garlic and seafood, adding shellfish in stages as needed.
Deglaze with wine and build the sauce.
Toss in the linguine and finish with parsley.

description: Ravioli ricotta are filled pasta pockets served with butter, sage, or tomato sauce depending on the style.
steps: Mix the ricotta filling until smooth and well seasoned.
Fill and seal the ravioli carefully.
Boil until the pasta is tender and the ravioli float.
Serve with sage butter or a light tomato sauce.

description: Pasta primavera is a light pasta dish with sauteed seasonal vegetables and a simple garlic or cream-light sauce.
steps: Cook the pasta and reserve some cooking water.
Saute the vegetables until just tender and still bright.
Add the pasta and a little cooking water to bring the sauce together.
Finish with parmesan and herbs.

description: Polenta with mushrooms is a northern Italian comfort dish of creamy cornmeal topped with sauteed mushrooms.
steps: Cook the polenta slowly until smooth and tender.
Saute the mushrooms with garlic and thyme.
Finish the polenta with butter and parmesan.
Serve the mushrooms spooned over the hot polenta.

description: Tiramisu bowl is a deconstructed version of tiramisu with coffee-soaked ladyfingers, mascarpone cream, and cocoa.
steps: Whisk the mascarpone mixture until smooth and airy.
Dip the ladyfingers briefly in coffee.
Layer coffee-soaked biscuits and mascarpone cream in bowls.
Chill if desired and dust with cocoa before serving.

description: Panzanella is a Tuscan bread salad with tomatoes, olive oil, vinegar, onion, and basil.
steps: Tear or cube the bread and moisten it lightly if very dry.
Mix with chopped tomatoes, onion, and basil.
Dress with olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Let it sit briefly so the bread absorbs the juices.

description: Mushroom risotto is a creamy rice dish where arborio rice is slowly cooked with stock and finished with sauteed mushrooms and cheese.
steps: Saute the mushrooms and reserve some for topping.
Cook shallot and toast the rice before adding stock gradually.
Stir frequently until the rice is tender but still slightly firm.
Finish with mushrooms, butter, and parmesan for a glossy texture.
